chart_metadata.name: rustfs
chart_metadata.description: RustFS is a high-performance, S3-compatible distributed object storage system written in Rust.

groups.name: RustFS Configuration
groups.description: Configure RustFS access credentials and behavior

groups.name: User and Group Configuration
groups.description: Configure User and Group for RustFS

attrs.label: Access Key
attrs.description:
S3 access key (acts as the admin username).</br>
Minimum 5 characters.

attrs.label: Secret Key
attrs.description:
S3 secret key (acts as the admin password).</br>
Minimum 8 characters.

attrs.label: Enable Web Console
attrs.description: Enable the RustFS web management console.

attrs.label: Server Domains (Optional)
attrs.description:
Comma-separated list of domains the server should respond to.</br>
Leave blank unless using virtual-hosted bucket addressing.

attrs.label: Additional Environment Variables
attrs.description:
Configure additional environment variables for RustFS.</br>
Use for TLS (RUSTFS_TLS_PATH), observability (RUSTFS_OBS_ENDPOINT), CORS, etc.

attrs.label: User ID
attrs.description:
The user id that RustFS will run as.</br>
Upstream container uses UID 10001; changing this requires the container to support it.

attrs.label: Group ID
attrs.description:
The group id that RustFS will run as.</br>
Upstream container uses GID 10001.

attrs.label: S3 API Port
attrs.description: The NodePort for the S3-compatible API.

attrs.label: Console Port
attrs.description: The NodePort for the web management console.

attrs.label: Host Network
attrs.description: Bind to the host network. It's recommended to keep this disabled.</br>

attrs.label: RustFS Data Storage
attrs.description:
Primary data volume for RustFS. Mounted to /data inside the container.</br>
Default uses host path /mnt/Pool0/RustFS/volume1.

attrs.label: Type
attrs.description:
ixVolume: Dataset created automatically by the system.</br>
Host Path: A path that already exists on the system.

enum.description: Host Path (Path that already exists on the system)
ixVolume (Dataset created automatically by the system)

attrs.label: ixVolume Configuration
attrs.description: The configuration for the ixVolume dataset.

attrs.label: Enable ACL
attrs.description: Enable ACL for the dataset.

attrs.label: Dataset Name
attrs.description: The name of the dataset to use for storage.

attrs.label: Path
attrs.description: Path to perform ACL

attrs.label: ACL Entries
attrs.description: ACL Entries

attrs.label: ID
attrs.description: Make sure to check the ID value is correct and aligns with RunAs user context of the application

attrs.label: Enable ACL
attrs.description: Enable ACL for the host path.

attrs.label: Host Path
attrs.description: Host Path to perform ACL

attrs.label: ACL Entries
attrs.description: ACL Entries

attrs.label: ID
attrs.description: Make sure to check the ID value is correct and aligns with RunAs user context of the application

attrs.label: Force Flag
attrs.description: Enabling `Force` applies ACL even if the path has existing data

attrs.label: Host Path
attrs.description: The host path to use for storage.

attrs.label: Additional Storage Mounts
attrs.description:
Extra paths mounted inside the container (logs, backups, manual locations).</br>
These are NOT additional RustFS data drives -- this chart runs in Single-Node Single-Disk (SNSD) mode.

attrs.label: Type
attrs.description:
ixVolume: Dataset created automatically by the system.</br>
Host Path: A path that already exists on the system.</br>
SMB Share: A SMB share mounted via persistent volume claim.

enum.description: Host Path (Path that already exists on the system)
ixVolume (Dataset created automatically by the system)
SMB Share (Mounts a persistent volume claim to a SMB share)

attrs.label: Read Only
attrs.description: Mount the volume as read only.

attrs.label: Mount Path
attrs.description: The path inside the container to mount the storage.

attrs.label: Enable ACL
attrs.description: Enable ACL for the host path.

attrs.label: Host Path
attrs.description: Host Path to perform ACL

attrs.label: ACL Entries
attrs.description: ACL Entries

attrs.label: ID
attrs.description: Make sure to check the ID value is correct and aligns with RunAs user context of the application

attrs.label: Force Flag
attrs.description: Enabling `Force` applies ACL even if the path has existing data

attrs.label: Host Path
attrs.description: The host path to use for storage.

attrs.label: ixVolume Configuration
attrs.description: The configuration for the ixVolume dataset.

attrs.label: Enable ACL
attrs.description: Enable ACL for the dataset.

attrs.label: Dataset Name
attrs.description: The name of the dataset to use for storage.

attrs.label: Path
attrs.description: Path to perform ACL

attrs.label: ACL Entries
attrs.description: ACL Entries

attrs.label: ID
attrs.description: Make sure to check the ID value is correct and aligns with RunAs user context of the application

attrs.label: SMB Share Configuration
attrs.description: The configuration for the SMB Share.

attrs.label: CPU
attrs.description: CPU limit for RustFS.

schema.valid_chars_error:
Valid CPU limit formats are</br>
- Plain Integer - eg. 1</br>
- Float - eg. 0.5</br>
- Milicpu - eg. 500m

attrs.label: Memory
attrs.description: Memory limit for RustFS.

schema.valid_chars_error:
Valid Memory limit formats are</br>
- Suffixed with E/P/T/G/M/K - eg. 1G</br>
- Suffixed with Ei/Pi/Ti/Gi/Mi/Ki - eg. 1Gi</br>
- Plain Integer in bytes - eg. 1024</br>
- Exponent - eg. 134e6

1.0.2.app_readme:
RustFS

RustFS is a high-performance, S3-compatible distributed object storage system written in Rust.

When the application is installed, a permissions init container will be launched with root privileges to chown mounted paths to UID/GID 10001 (RustFS upstream non-root user). The main container then runs as a non-root user.

Deployment Mode

This chart runs RustFS in Single-Node Single-Disk (SNSD) mode. One data volume is mounted to /data. SNSD provides no erasure coding -- redundancy must come from the underlying storage (e.g. ZFS raidz/mirror).

For multi-drive or distributed deployments, deploy RustFS outside this chart.

Default Storage Path

The default data path is /mnt/Pool0/RustFS/volume1. Create the parent dataset before deploying, or switch the data storage type to ixVolume to have TrueNAS create the dataset automatically.
1.0.2.detailed_readme:
RustFS Chart

S3-compatible object storage server (https://github.com/rustfs/rustfs) packaged for TrueNAS SCALE.

Configuration

| Parameter | Default | Notes | |---|---|---| | image.tag | 1.0.0-beta.8 | Pinned upstream tag | | rustfsConfig.accessKey | (required) | S3 access key, min 5 chars | | rustfsConfig.secretKey | (required) | S3 secret key, min 8 chars | | rustfsConfig.consoleEnable | true | Web console enabled | | rustfsNetwork.apiPort | 30100 | S3 API NodePort | | rustfsNetwork.consolePort | 30101 | Console NodePort | | rustfsRunAs.user/group | 10001 | Upstream container user | | rustfsStorage.data.hostPathConfig.hostPath | /mnt/Pool0/RustFS/volume1 | Default data path | | resources.limits.cpu | 4000m | CPU ceiling | | resources.limits.memory | 8Gi | Memory ceiling |

Mode

Single-Node Single-Disk (SNSD). No erasure coding. Underlying ZFS provides redundancy.

Additional Storage

rustfsStorage.additionalStorages mounts extra paths inside the container (logs, backups, etc.). These are not RustFS data drives.

TLS

Not surfaced in the form. To enable, set RUSTFS_TLS_PATH via additionalEnvs and mount certs via additionalStorages at the matching container path.

Configuration
